claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

First, I need to know about the child (or children) you'd like to claim Child Benefit for.

**How old is your child?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, we can go through each one, but let's start with the first child. What is their name and age?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex who is 8 and Blake who is 9. They both live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! It's great that you have Alex (8) and Blake (9) living with you. Since both children are under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit.

Let me continue with the assessment. I'll ask questions that apply to both children together, since their situations seem similar.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Given their ages (8 and 9), I'd expect neither of them are working, but I just need to confirm this for the assessment. Are both children either not working at all, or working less than 24 hours per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work at all - they're just at primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected! Since both Alex and Blake are in primary school and not working, they meet this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are Alex and Blake NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means neither child is receiving benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own name. 

Given their ages, this is very unlikely, but I just need to confirm - are both children free from receiving these types of benefits in their own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - neither of them receives any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming that. Let's continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are Alex and Blake NOT in any apprenticeships?**

Since they're 8 and 9 years old and in primary school, I assume neither of them is in an apprenticeship. Can you confirm that neither child is currently doing an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not in any apprenticeships - they're just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that makes sense! Since both children are in primary school and not in any apprenticeships, they meet this requirement too.

Now I need to ask some questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you need to meet one of the following residency criteria. You only need to meet **one** of these:

1. **You normally live in the UK** - this is the most common situation
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do you normally live in the UK, or do any of these other situations apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you! Living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are you responsible for Alex and Blake?**

You mentioned earlier that both children live at home with you, which is great! To confirm, responsibility for a child can be shown by meeting **any one** of these criteria:

1. **Living with the child** - the child lives with you
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - you pay at least the Child Benefit amount (or equivalent) towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - you make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

Since you said Alex and Blake both live at home with you, can you confirm that you're responsible for them (i.e., they live with you and you look after them)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely - they both live with me at home and I look after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That confirms you're responsible for both Alex and Blake. Let's continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

Child Benefit can only be paid to one person per child. I need to check:

- Is there **no one else** claiming Child Benefit for these children?
- Or if someone else has been claiming, have they **agreed to transfer** the claim to you?
- Or is there a **dispute** about who should receive it?

Are you the only person claiming (or planning to claim) Child Benefit for Alex and Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them - it's just me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's straightforward. Let's continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Where are Alex and Blake currently living?**

I need to check if any special circumstances apply to where the children are staying. Are they:

- **In hospital** (for an extended stay)
- **In local authority care**
- **Being fostered**
- **Living normally at home with you** (the usual situation)

Since you mentioned they live at home with you, can you confirm they are living at home under normal circumstances (not in hospital, care, or fostering arrangements)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - they're both living at home with me under normal circumstances. They're not in hospital, care, or being fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming that. Just one more check to go!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question: Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex and Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

To be eligible for Child Benefit, the local council must not be paying for the children's accommodation or maintenance costs.

Can you confirm that you're covering their living costs yourself, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - I'm covering all their living costs myself. The local council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful news! You've completed the assessment and met all the eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Great News - You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

Based on our assessment, you are **eligible** to claim Child Benefit for both **Alex** and **Blake**.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| Alex | 8 | ✅ Yes |
| Blake | 9 | ✅ Yes |

### Why You're Eligible
Both children meet all the requirements:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in their own right
- ✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ You are responsible for them (they live with you)
- ✅ No one else is claiming for them
- ✅ Local council is not paying for their care

### Next Steps to Claim

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** for Alex and Blake

### Important Information

- **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may have to pay back some or all of the benefit through tax. You can still claim but should be aware of this.

### Useful Link
👉 [How to Claim Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 8) qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) They are not working, (3) They do not receive benefits in their own right, (4) They are not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) The claimant lives in the UK, (6) The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (8) Alex is living at home under normal circumstances, and (9) The local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 9) qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) They are not working, (3) They do not receive benefits in their own right, (4) They are not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, (5) The claimant lives in the UK, (6) The claimant is responsible for Blake as they live together, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Blake is living at home under normal circumstances, and (9) The local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children. They are a UK resident living in Manchester, responsible for both Alex (8) and Blake (9) who live at home with them. Both children are under 16, in primary school, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and the local council is not contributing to their care.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child
